#[derive(Debug)]
enum BuilderError {
    MissingHost,
    TlsRequiresCert,
    TlsRequiresKey,
    InvalidPort,
}

struct ServerConfig {
    host: String,
    port: u16,
    max_connections: u32,
    tls_enabled: bool,
    cert_path: Option<String>,
    key_path: Option<String>,
}

struct ServerConfigBuilder {
    host: Option<String>,
    port: Option<u16>,
    max_connections: Option<u32>,
    tls_enabled: Option<bool>,
    cert_path: Option<String>,
    key_path: Option<String>,
}

impl ServerConfigBuilder {
    fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            host: None,
            port: None,
            max_connections: None,
            tls_enabled: None,
            cert_path: None,
            key_path: None,
        }
    }

    fn host(mut self, host: String) -> Self { self.host = Some(host); self }
    fn port(mut self, port: u16) -> Self { self.port = Some(port); self }
    fn max_connections(mut self, n: u32) -> Self { self.max_connections = Some(n); self }
    fn tls_enabled(mut self, v: bool) -> Self { self.tls_enabled = Some(v); self }
    fn cert_path(mut self, p: String) -> Self { self.cert_path = Some(p); self }
    fn key_path(mut self, p: String) -> Self { self.key_path = Some(p); self }

    fn build(self) -> Result<ServerConfig, BuilderError> {
        let host = self.host.ok_or(BuilderError::MissingHost)?;
        let port = self.port.unwrap_or(8080);
        if port == 0 {
            return Err(BuilderError::InvalidPort);
        }
        let tls = self.tls_enabled.unwrap_or(false);
        if tls && self.cert_path.is_none() {
            return Err(BuilderError::TlsRequiresCert);
        }
        if tls && self.key_path.is_none() {
            return Err(BuilderError::TlsRequiresKey);
        }
        Ok(ServerConfig {
            host,
            port,
            max_connections: self.max_connections.unwrap_or(100),
            tls_enabled: tls,
            cert_path: self.cert_path,
            key_path: self.key_path,
        })
    }
}
